cnv.name: cnv
cnv.note: ROOT-CAUSED (fix deferred): PaddingBlocks (elided/boundary blocks + region separators) renders AFTER TrackRenderingContainer in TrackContainer.tsx to mask the track canvas at inter-region positions. The MultiWiggle OverlayColorLegend is inside the display SVG, which is trapped in trackRenderingContainer contain:strict (own stacking context), so PaddingBlocks paints over it. TrackLabel avoids this via zIndex:200 but is a generic TrackContainer child; the legend can not escape the contain:strict from inside. Whole-genome hg19 shows unplaced-contig elided blocks at the far right, coinciding with the top-right legend. Clean fix = render wiggle legend chrome as a TrackContainer-level overlay above PaddingBlocks (portal or overlay-slot); shared LGV arch + needs jbrowse-web rebuild+visual verify.

alignments/modifications2.name: alignments/modifications2
alignments/modifications2.note: Reworked into a two-stage figure: stage 1 shows the live "Color by..." menu with "Color by methylation" and "Color by modification type" boxed (the actual choice point), stage 2 is the existing two-row comparison with callouts moved to the right side of each track (previously left-anchored, overlapping the CpG island / hypomethylated block the callouts describe). Root-caused a real bug in the process: the annotation SVG overlay is sized to the live page viewport (100vh), not the post-capture crop -- a spec that raises crop without also raising viewportHeight silently clips any annotation below the viewport height. Fixed by adding a matching viewportHeight. Also boxed the "Color by..." parent item itself (not just its two children) per follow-up request.

sv_cgiab/cnv_log2_baf.note: browser window needs to be taller, resolution needs to be higher for the log2 track. refer to other screenshot though we may need to wait until labserver is available

sv_cgiab/cnv_log2ratio_genome.name: sv_cgiab/cnv_log2ratio_genome
sv_cgiab/cnv_log2ratio_genome.note: the multiwiggle depth should ideally be raw depths not indexcov. can use aws cli to 'aws s3 ls' in cgiab demos folder if it helps. we can also consider using a production grade cnv detector like cnvpytor or an R tool or something to get the best signal to plot. we may not want to do that now because we cant access our labserver to run analysis. we may want to make this screenshot wider. the baf values are also probably confusing for anyone without proper background, and the 'whiskers' are confusing too. also we are not plotting folded b-allele frequency anymore

sv_cgiab/driver_cdkn2a_deletion.name: sv_cgiab/driver_cdkn2a_deletion
sv_cgiab/driver_cdkn2a_deletion.note: Applied heightMode:fit so the ~116x pileup packs into the fixed height without clipping. "Sort split reads to bottom" was not applied -- confirmed with the user there is no sort/group-by option that supports it while linkedReads chain mode is on (a chain's members must share one group key; "is-part-of-a-chain" is not a groupable dimension).

maf_codon_tooltip.name: maf_codon_tooltip

gc_content.name: gc_content
gc_content.note: FIXED: root-caused — LinearGCContentTrackDisplay/LinearGCContentDisplay inherited summaryScoreMode:whiskers from LinearWiggleDisplay, and buildSourceRenderData skips the bicolor pos/neg split entirely in whiskers mode (posColor only), so GC skew rendered monochrome despite useBicolor/bicolorPivot already defaulting correctly. GCContentAdapter never emits real per-bin min/max, so whiskers mode was pointless there anyway — defaulted summaryScoreMode to avg in both display config schemas. Skew now shows the real two-arm blue/red replichore profile. Replication-fork annotation left as a follow-up.

sv_cgiab/driver_kras_gain.name: sv_cgiab/driver_kras_gain
sv_cgiab/driver_kras_gain.note: FIXED: viewportHeight 780->880 so the CN-labeled CNV calls track has clearance below it instead of being cropped at the frame edge.

display_settings_url_snapshot.name: display_settings_url_snapshot
display_settings_url_snapshot.note: The blank-white capture was a stale/broken render -- a plain --force regen against the current build now renders the volvox_sv_cram pileup correctly (colored SV cluster on the left, matching the doc's caption). Kept volvox rather than swapping to human data: the doc's code block right above the figure shows the literal volvox_sv_cram JSON verbatim, so the figure needs to match that exact session, not a different track.

Fixed: karyotype-ordered main-chromosome-only chrom.sizes for both hg19+hg38 (test_data), autoDiagonalize off, config sample-data cleaned too. Clean diagonal, natural chr1..22,X,Y on both axes, alt-contig clutter gone. Spec now uses sessionSpec() helper + plain JSON.
